title: TUI run_turn channel-closed errors discard partial work
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/pipeline.rs:19-37, 526-633
## Violation
Two macros (send_event! and send_event_or_break!) both panic-style return when the mpsc Sender is closed. send_event! returns Err("TUI channel closed") from stream_sse_response, discarding the accumulated full_content, stream_usage, and any tool_calls collected so far. The caller that observed a channel-closed error has no way to drain that work. Worse, the macros are not consistent: send_event! returns Err while send_event_or_break! breaks the loop. The function ends up returning Err with no chance to persist tokens consumed by the upstream provider — billing event silently lost.
## Mandated refactor
Replace string-typed errors with a TurnError enum (ChannelClosed { partial: TurnResult }, ProviderError, ...). On ChannelClosed return the partial TurnResult so the caller can record usage and clean up. Collapse the two macros into one helper that returns a Result<(), ChannelClosed>.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
